Name: Cody Rice-Velasquez | DOB: 5/18/1990 | MRN: 3332015 | PCP: | Legal Name: Cody Rice-Velasquez Emergency Department - May 15, 2026 at Deaconess Midtown Hospital Emergency Department Notes from Care Team CHIEF COMPLAINT HPI Cody Rice-Velasquez is a 35 y.o. adult who presents with me. Symptoms starting over an hour ago. The patient woke up this morning with uncontrollable diarrhea and nausea but no vomiting. The patient was little warm but never spiked a fever. There was no runny nose or cough but the patient did have tingling in pain shooting down his entire body from his torso down to his legs followed by a possible syncopal episode. Now he points to the chest and says that he has been experiencing pain and chest as well. There was no change in vision or any other symptom. Vital signs are normal on arrival. He weighs 230 lb. There is no current pain. The patient has no significant past medical history but has had panic attacks in the past. He's just never come to the hospital for this. I believe he called 911. REVIEW OF SYSTEMS All other systems are negative. PAST MEDICAL HISTORY History reviewed. No pertinent past medical history. FAMILY HISTORY No family history on file. # Page 6^page=6
MyChart® licensed from Epic Systems Corporation© 1999 - 2026 Please see the nursing note for further documentation. The patient says that this is almost certainly anxiety because of a high degree of stress. He is the primary care giver for his mother and also earns extra money working for a ride share company. Pertinent Labs, Imaging Studies, and EKG were reviewed as above. CBC is unremarkable. CMP is normal. The patient has no alcohol in his serum. D-dimer is undetectable. The patient is at low risk for pulmonary embolus DVT and dissection. Troponin is undetectable. Chest x-ray shows no portable plain film evidence of acute cardiopulmonary disease. I consulted the psychiatrist. The patient will be discharged and he will be given outpatient resources in counseling and will be put back on his psychiatric medications. FINAL IMPRESSION 1. Nausea and diarrhea 2. Chest pain 3. Panic attack Andrew L.P. Houseman, MD, MD Houseman, Andrew L., MD 05/15/26 1434 FEU 5/22/26, 1:03 PM MyChart - Past Visit Details https://www.viewmychart.com/MyChart/app/visits/past-details?csn=WP-247LPH8NCkYDC7UGet0LCURA-3D-3D-24PWJstMasOGJBeLojCh5D8ktVZ… 6/6
